title: SELECT COUNT(*) using multi-valued index reports wrong number of rows.
description:
COUNT(*) query report wrong number of rows when using multi-valued index.

> SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_mvi;
+----------+
| COUNT(*) |
+----------+
|   100000 |
+----------+

> SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_mvi FORCE INDEX (PRIMARY) WHERE type='T1';
+----------+
| COUNT(*) |
+----------+
|    97998 |
+----------+

> SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_mvi FORCE INDEX (ix_type_hid_pat) WHERE type='T1';
+----------+
| COUNT(*) |
+----------+
|   391989 |
+----------+
How to repeat:
You can reproduce this bug using the attached file of following bug report.
https://bugs.mysql.com/bug.php?id=104897
poc: CREATE TABLE test_mvi (id INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, type VARCHAR(10), hid INT, pat VARCHAR(10), INDEX ix_type_hid_pat (type, hid, pat));
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_mvi FORCE INDEX (PRIMARY) WHERE type='T1';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_mvi FORCE INDEX (ix_type_hid_pat) WHERE type='T1';
key_bug_features: COUNT(*) query with multi-valued index returns incorrect results.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_mvi (id INT NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY, type VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL, hid INT DEFAULT NULL, pat VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT NULL, INDEX ix_type_hid_pat (type, hid, pat));
INSERT INTO test_mvi (type, hid, pat) VALUES ('T1', 1, 'A'), ('T1', 2, 'B'), ('T2', 3, 'C'), ('T1', 4, 'D'), ('T1', NULL, 'E');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_mvi WHERE hid IS NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_mvi WHERE type='T1' AND hid > 0;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_mvi FORCE INDEX (ix_type_hid_pat) WHERE type='T1' OR pat IS NOT NULL;
